scenario_description: The user first asks to sort by January ascending, then changes their mind and wants February descending instead.

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Help me sort by the January column from smallest to largest
Changed my mind. Sort by the February column from largest to smallest instead. Undo the previous sort first, then save

result.command: python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_excel('/home/user/Desktop/SalesRep.xlsx'); feb_cols = [c for c in df.columns if 'feb' in str(c).lower()]; ok = len(feb_cols) > 0 and df[feb_cols[0]].is_monotonic_decreasing; print('PASSED' if ok else 'FAILED')"
